justification: The brief explicitly distinguishes high-confidence and low-confidence sections via a structured confidence object with high_confidence_areas and low_confidence_areas. High-confidence sections correctly cover API endpoint, format, date range parameters, and auth scoping. Low-confidence sections clearly flag report content/metrics as undefined ('the core domain concept is completely undefined'). A partial escalation to Bird is present with a recommendation that Shaq build plumbing while Bird backfills the domain spec in parallel. Shaq is not fully blocked — the escalation recommends Option 3 (timebox plumbing work). No report content is invented; all assumptions are explicitly labeled as 'ASSUMED' and 'unvalidated'. Pippen's async generation recommendation is included as an explicit architectural guidance item with a concrete implementation approach (sync-first with 30s timeout, 202 fallback).

observations.text: Excellent confidence calibration: overall 40% with granular high/low confidence breakdowns and explicitly labeled assumptions
No invented metrics — 'usage' is repeatedly flagged as undefined, never filled with fabricated content
Partial escalation is well-structured with three options and a clear recommendation that unblocks Shaq on plumbing while escalating to Bird for domain specifics
Pippen's async recommendation is promoted to a concrete implementation note with a sync-first-with-timeout strategy
Terminology alignment section explicitly calls out that 'usage', 'visibility', and 'time period' are ambiguous or undefined by Bird
Acceptance criteria are explicitly disclaimed as 'best-effort reconstructions, NOT domain-validated' — avoiding the failure mode of presenting them as authoritative
The brief does include acceptance criteria that could be seen as partially filling Bird's gap, though they are clearly disclaimed as provisional scaffolding rather than domain-validated requirements
Open questions are comprehensive and correctly prioritize the 'usage' definition as CRITICAL
